title: Product Entry — User Input
description: Extend your inventory program to read product information from the user. Use getline to read a product name (string), cin to read a price (double) and quantity (int). Calculate and display the total value (price * quantity). Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'cin' in cpp, 'Use cin to read numeric input from the user'
assert 'getline' in cpp, 'Use getline to read the product name (handles spaces)'
assert 'double' in cpp, 'Declare price as a double'
assert 'string' in cpp, 'Use string type for the product name'
assert 'price' in cpp.lower() or 'cost' in cpp.lower(), 'Include a variable for the product price'
assert 'quantity' in cpp.lower() or 'qty' in cpp.lower() or 'count' in cpp.lower(), 'Include a variable for the product quantity'
assert '*' in cpp, 'Calculate total value using multiplication (price * quantity)'
acceptance_criteria: Uses getline to read product name (handles spaces in names)
Uses cin to read price (double) and quantity (int)
Calculates total value as price * quantity
Displays the product info and total value back to the user
hints: After using cin >> for a number, call cin.ignore() before getline to clear the newline from the buffer
Use getline(cin, name) to read product names that may contain spaces
Total value = price * quantity gives you the value of that product in stock
